title: The Premier League players attracting loan interest from the Championship in this transfer window
text: The transfer window is in full swing with Championship clubs actively pursuing Premier League players available on loan. Sky Sports examined several prospects who could move to the second tier for the remainder of the season. Toby Collyer - The Manchester United midfielder spent early season on loan at West Brom before a calf injury prompted his recall. Hull City now pursues a loan deal, with head coach Sergej Jakirovic confirming his interest. Multiple Championship clubs want Collyer, who has been sidelined since November but nears full fitness. Harry Amass - After impressing during his Sheffield Wednesday loan with 21 appearances, the Manchester United left-back returned to his parent club. Wednesday manager Henrik Pedersen expressed strong desire to bring Amass back to Hillsborough for the remainder of the campaign. The teenager became a fan favorite, scoring once in a 3-1 Southampton defeat. Joel Piroe - Last season's Championship top scorer has struggled for minutes at Leeds United in the Premier League, with no starts since August's 5-0 Arsenal loss. Despite Championship interest domestically and abroad, Piroe has shown no desire to leave Leeds, and the club won't push him out due to PSR constraints limiting January activity. Fer Lopez - Wolves' £19 million Spanish playmaker from Celta Vigo has appeared in only nine Premier League matches with just two starts. A Championship loan could provide valuable minutes as he adapts to English football, particularly relevant given Wolves' relegation risk from bottom placement. Jesurun Rak-Sakyi - Crystal Palace recalled the winger from his Turkish loan this month. Swansea City manager Vitor Matos called Rak-Sakyi really interesting, with other Championship clubs monitoring his situation. He demonstrated Championship capability last season with Sheffield United, contributing seven goals and two assists across 34 league appearances. David Datro Fofana - The Chelsea striker currently plays in Turkey for Fatih Karagümrük. Derby County and Celtic expressed interest, though his Deadline Day Charlton move failed due to administrative issues. Chelsea seeks to free an international loan spot through permanent or domestic deals. Dan Neil - The Sunderland midfielder captained his hometown club to playoff success but has made only three Premier League appearances this season. Growing expectations suggest his January departure, with Sheffield United among interested Championship clubs. Zepiqueno Redmond - Aston Villa recalled the teenage striker from his Huddersfield loan after injury interrupted his spell. The 18-year-old, who arrived from Feyenoord with significant pedigree including Champions League experience and U19 Euros success, will likely loan to Championship or League One for the second half of the season pending recovery. Louie Barry - The Aston Villa forward returned from a disappointing Sheffield United loan in January. While he showed Stockport County brilliance, he struggled with consistency and knee issues. Villa reportedly plans another Championship loan, with multiple suitors interested despite recent difficulties.
